unspecified_in_paper.energy_unit_note: E_uav=2000 treated as abstract unit; all energies on consistent dimensionless scale
unspecified_in_paper.exploration_noise: Gaussian with linear sigma decay across training
unspecified_in_paper.episode_termination: fixed T=40 slots; energy-depleted UAV becomes inactive (action ignored)
unspecified_in_paper.boundary_violation: position clipped to [0,l_max] and penalty applied
unspecified_in_paper.ddpg_baseline_variant: shared policy across UAVs, each agent uses local observation only
unspecified_in_paper.greedy_collision_handling: if target SD causes collision, fall back to next-largest L_k SD
unspecified_in_paper.energy_scale_note: paper's energy formulas produce values up to ~1e17 per slot vs E_uav=2000. We divide raw energy by energy_scale to bring per-slot energy to ~1-50 unit so an episode of T=40 slots is feasible.
unspecified_in_paper.movement_energy_mult_note: paper's E_oper movement portion (~1e3) is ~10^9 smaller than E_com (~1e12). We multiply the movement-only part of E_oper to make trajectory choices energy-relevant.
unspecified_in_paper.use_uav_queue_note: v6 toggle. If true, served SDs transfer their queue into a per-UAV buffer; per-slot throughput is capped by f (bits/slot). If false, original v5 behavior (entire SD queue cleared instantly on service, f only affects energy).
unspecified_in_paper.slot_capacity_mode_note: How f maps to per-slot bit-processing capacity when use_uav_queue=true. 'identity' -> capacity = f (bits/slot); with f_max=1e6 and task_mean=1e6, that gives ~1 task/slot at f_max and ~0.01 task/slot at f_min, making f choice strongly affect throughput.
unspecified_in_paper.uav_queue_max_note: Cap on per-UAV processing buffer (bits). Only used when use_uav_queue=true. Set to f_max * k (k slots worth). Forces UAV to keep moving to replenish queue instead of front-loading and stopping. 0 or null = no cap (v6 original behavior).
